content_en:
Shubrum is among the medications that doctors do not advise people to use because it is a very strong

laxative. In the Haditb, the Prophet sg described Shubrum as being hot and a strong laxative.

Senna is a plant that grows in AI-Hijaz (Western Arabia) and the best kind of it grows in Mecca, Senna is hot and dry in tue first degree and it is a good, mild medication that does not cause any side effects. Senno helps against bile and black bile ailments and strengthens the heart, which is another good quality that this medication contains. Senno is useful against melancholic obsession and corporal incisions, relaxes the muscles and improves the hair. Senno In addition helps against lice, headache, mange, pustules, rashes and epilepsy. It is better to cook the Senno whole and drink three measures of its soup than cooking it crushed. In addition, it is better to cook the Senno mixed with violet flowers and red raisins, after removing the seeds,

Ar-Razi said, "Senna and fumitory help extract mature humors (bodily wastes) and helps against leprosy and rashes, when one takes four to seven measures of each remedy."

As for Sonoot (cumin), there is a difference of opinion concerning its nature. Umar bin Bakr As-Saksaki, for instance, said that Sonoot is either honey or butter extract. Another opinion suggests that Sonoot is a seed that resembles the Kammon (cumin), as Ibn Al-Arabi has asserted. The fourth and fifth opinions suggest that

——-— The Prophetic Mecicine

Sonoot is the Persian cumin or fennel. according to Abu Hanifah Ad-Daynocri. The sixth opinion sugyests that it is the Shibitt (dill), and the seventh opinion suggests that it is the date, according to Abu Bakr bin As-Sunni, The eighth opinion suggests that it is honey that exists in butter-skin, according to Abdul-Latif Al-Baghdadi, Some doctors In addition stated that the last meaning is the most probable. In this case, the Sanoot is prepared by blending Senno powder with some honey mixed with ghee, as this is better than taking the Senna by itself. Then one licks the mixture that contains the Senna, as in this case the Senna taste will be made milder, because of the honey and butter, which will In addition assist the laxative power of the Senna. Allah knows best.

The Prophet's guidance on treating skin rashes and scabies caused by mites

In the Sahihan it is narrated that Anas said, "The Messenger of Allah b has allowed Abdur Rahman bin Awf and Az-Zubayr bin Al'Awwam to wear silk garments because of a skin rash they had." fn another narration, "Abdur Rahman bin Awf and Az-Zubayr bin Al-Awwam complained to the Prophet b during a battle that they had mites, and he allowed them to wear silk garments which I saw them wearing."

There are two areas of interest regarding this Hadith, one in the area of Figh (islamic Jurisprudence)

In the area of Figh (Islamic Jurisprudence), the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah is that wearing silken garments is allowed for women and disallowed for men, except when there is a legitimate need. For instance, [men are allowed to wear silken garments] in cold weather, when one-has ors a sitken garment to wear and when complaining from mange, rashes, mites or lice, as evident by the last Hadith.

According to Imam Ahmad and Shafi'i, wearing silken garments is allowed {for men if a necessity warrants it]. The permission given to some Muslims {such as wearing silken garments for men in this case] due to special circumstances applies te all those who face similar circumstances.

Several scholars said that the Ahadeeth that disallow silken garments for men apply in general. They said that the Hadith (narrated above) might entail a specific permission for only Abdur-Rahman bin '‘Awf and Az-Zubayr. They say that it is possible, though, that this permission applies to other Muslims. When there is a probability, adhering to the general text is warranted. This is why some of the narrators of this Hadith commented, "I do not know if the permission applies to whoever comes after them (Abdur-Rahman bin Awf and Az-Zubayr)."

——— The Prophetic Medicine

The correct opinion is that the permission is general in its indication and that there is no evidence that it is specific or restricted. Similarly, the Prophet st once said to Abu Burdah, "This permission applies to you and none else after you." In addition, Allah said to His Prophet * regarding the woman who offered herself in marriage to the Prophet b:

"A privilege for you only, not for the (rest of) the believers."

Since disallowing wearing silken garments for men is a precautionary measure, it is allowed in certain situations and for a benefit that warrants it. For instance, gazing at women is disallowed as a precautionary measure, but it is allowed when there is a real need. Such is the case with disallowing performing voluntary prayers at certain times while the sun is rising or setting], to prevent even a hint of resemblance between Muslims and sun worshippers. Yet, in some cases, it is allowed for Muslims to offer voluntary prayers during these times for a real need and benefit. We have explained the cases when wearing silken garments is allowed in our book on this subject, At-Jabiri.

Medically, silk is produced by an animal and isa remedy. Silk has many benefits, such as soothing and strengthening the heart and helping relieve several of its ailments. Silk In addition helps against black bile and

whatever ailments it might cause. Further, silk strengthens the eyesight when used as eyeliner. Raw silk, which is used in preparing medications and remedies, is hot and dry in the first degree. When silk is used in clothes, it is mild and heats the body. Sometimes it could chill the body.

Ar-Razi said, "Silk is hotter than linen, colder than cotton and develops the flesh. Every type of thick clothes weakens the body and hardens the skin."

There are three types of clothes, one that brings warmth and In addition heats the body. Another type of clothes brings warmth but no heat to the body. The third type does not bring warmth or heat. There is no type that brings heat but not warmth to those wearing it. For instance, clothes made of wool and animal hair elevate body temperature and bring warmth to the skin. On the other hand, silk, Kittan (linen) and cotton garments only bring warmth to the skin. Kittan clothes are cold and dry, wool clothes are hot and dry, while cotton garments are moderate. Silken garments are softer and less hot than wool. The author of Al-Minhaj stated that wearing silk, "Does not bring as much warmth as cotton, because it is milder."

Every type of soft, polished clothes heat the body less and is less effective in helping the decomposition process. That is why it is better that this type of clothes is

———— The Prophetic Medicine

worn during summer and in hot areas.

Since silk.n garments are neither dry nor thick as the other types of clothes, they help as a treatment for skin rashes that result from dry, thick material. Hence, the Prophet “ allowed Az-Zubair and Abdur-Rahman to wear silken garments due to their skin rash. In addition, silken garments are the least hospitable to mites or lice, for it is not the best environment where mites live and thrive.

The types of body wear that neither elevates the body temperature nor brings warmth are made of iron, lead, wood, sand, and so forth.

If one asks, "Since silk provides the best type of clothes and the most suitable for the body, why was it prohibited for men) by the Islamic legislation, which is the mot honorable, perfect legislation and which allows the good and pure things and only disallows the impure things?" We should mention that this is a question that the Muslims differ regarding its answer.

Those who deny that there is wisdom behind the Islamic legislation do not even need to answer this question.

As for those who affirm that there is a wisdom behind the Islamic legislation, who are the majority, say that Islam disallowed silken garments for men so that they

observe patience and abandon wearing them for Allah's Sake. In this case, they will be rewarded by Allah, especially since there are other types of clothes to wear.

Some people say that silk was created for the benefit of women, such as the case with gold. Hence, silk is prohibited for men so that they do not imitate women. Some Muslims say that silk was prohibited because it leads to arrogance and pride.

Others say that silk was disallowed because of its sofiness on the skin that leads to feminine behavior while weakening manhed and masculinity. Hence, you rarely see a man wearing silken garments who is not affected by its softness by inadvertently imitating feminine behavior and softness, even if he was among the most masculine men. Wearing silken garments will certainly diminish the manly qualities and masculinity, although these qualities will not disappear all together. As for those who do not comprehend these facts, let them submit to Allah's wise decisions. Finally, we should state that according to the correct opinion, young boys should not be allowed to wear silken garments, because they will acquire feminine behavior.

An-Nasa'y narrated that the Prophet b said:

"Allah has allowed silk and gold for the females of my Ummah (Muslim Nation) and disallowed them for the

——— The Prophetic Medicine

males of my Ummah (Muslim Nation)." In another narration, the Prophet b said:

"Wearing silk and gold is disallowed for the males of my Nation and allowed for the females."

Al-Bokhary In addition narrated that the Messenger of Allah b has disallowed wearing and sitting on silken garments and Dibaj (pure silken clothes), and then added, "It is for them (whoever wears them among men) in this life and for you in the Life Hereafter."

The Prophet's guidance on treating pleurisy

The doctors divide pleurisy into two types, real and unreal. The first type is an infected growth that appears in the pleura that coats the ribs. The second type causes a similar pain and attacks the pleura as a result of thick, congested flatulence between the peritoneum's (smooth transparent membrane that lines the abdominal cavity and part of the pelvic region). The pain caused by the unreal pleurisy is acute while the real pleurisy causes stinging pain.

The author of the Qonoon said, "Sometimes, the flank (of the body), the peritoneum, chest muscles and ribs and surrounding areas suffer from an extremely painful growth called pleurisy. Sometimes, there could be pain in these same areas of the body but not as a result of

growth but of accumulating harmful gasses (flatulence),
content_id:
Shubrum termasuk obat-obatan yang tidak disarankan oleh para dokter untuk digunakan karena merupakan pencahar yang sangat kuat. Dalam hadith tersebut, Nabi ﷺ menggambarkan Shubrum sebagai obat yang panas dan pencahar yang kuat.

Senna adalah tanaman yang tumbuh di Al-Hijaz (Arabia Barat) dan jenis terbaiknya tumbuh di Mekah. Senna bersifat panas dan kering pada tingkat pertama, dan merupakan obat yang baik serta ringan yang tidak menimbulkan efek samping. Senna membantu mengatasi penyakit empedu dan empedu hitam serta memperkuat jantung, yang merupakan kualitas baik lainnya yang terkandung dalam obat ini. Senna bermanfaat untuk mengatasi obsesi melankolis dan luka pada tubuh, melemaskan otot-otot dan memperbaiki rambut. Senna juga membantu mengatasi kutu, sakit kepala, kudis, bisul, ruam dan epilepsi. Lebih baik memasak Senna secara utuh dan meminum tiga takaran air rebusannya daripada memasaknya dalam keadaan ditumbuk. Selain itu, lebih baik memasak Senna dicampur dengan bunga violet dan kismis merah, setelah bijinya dibuang.

Ar-Razi berkata, "Senna dan fumitori membantu mengeluarkan cairan tubuh yang matang (limbah tubuh) dan membantu mengatasi lepra dan ruam, ketika seseorang mengonsumsi empat hingga tujuh takaran dari masing-masing obat."

Adapun Sanoot (jintan), terdapat perbedaan pendapat mengenai sifatnya. Umar bin Bakr As-Saksaki, misalnya, mengatakan bahwa Sanoot adalah madu atau ekstrak mentega. Pendapat lain menyebutkan bahwa Sanoot adalah biji yang menyerupai Kammon (jintan), sebagaimana ditegaskan oleh Ibnu Al-Arabi. Pendapat keempat dan kelima menyebutkan bahwa

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

Sanoot adalah jintan Persia atau adas, menurut Abu Hanifah Ad-Daynuri. Pendapat keenam menyebutkan bahwa itu adalah Shibitt (dill), dan pendapat ketujuh menyebutkan bahwa itu adalah kurma, menurut Abu Bakr bin As-Sunni. Pendapat kedelapan menyebutkan bahwa itu adalah madu yang terdapat dalam kantong mentega, menurut Abdul-Latif Al-Baghdadi. Beberapa dokter juga menyatakan bahwa makna terakhir inilah yang paling mungkin. Dalam hal ini, Sanoot disiapkan dengan mencampurkan bubuk Senna dengan sedikit madu yang dicampur dengan ghee, karena ini lebih baik daripada mengonsumsi Senna sendirian. Kemudian seseorang menjilat campuran yang mengandung Senna tersebut, karena dalam hal ini rasa Senna akan menjadi lebih ringan berkat madu dan mentega, yang juga akan membantu kekuatan pencahar dari Senna. Allah Maha Mengetahui.

Petunjuk Nabi dalam mengobati ruam kulit dan kudis yang disebabkan oleh tungau

Dalam Shahihain diriwayatkan bahwa Anas berkata, "Rasulullah ﷺ mengizinkan Abdurrahman bin Awf dan Az-Zubayr bin Al-Awwam untuk mengenakan pakaian sutra karena ruam kulit yang mereka derita." Dalam riwayat lain, "Abdurrahman bin Awf dan Az-Zubayr bin Al-Awwam mengadu kepada Nabi ﷺ saat peperangan bahwa mereka terkena tungau, maka beliau mengizinkan mereka mengenakan pakaian sutra yang kulihat mereka memakainya."

Ada dua aspek yang menarik mengenai hadith ini, satu dalam bidang fiqh (Yurisprudensi Islam).

Dalam bidang fiqh (Yurisprudensi Islam), Sunnah Rasulullah adalah bahwa mengenakan pakaian sutra dibolehkan bagi wanita dan dilarang bagi laki-laki, kecuali ketika ada kebutuhan yang sah. Misalnya, [laki-laki dibolehkan mengenakan pakaian sutra] dalam cuaca dingin, ketika seseorang hanya memiliki pakaian sutra untuk dipakai, dan ketika mengalami kudis, ruam, tungau atau kutu, sebagaimana terbukti dari hadith terakhir.

Menurut Imam Ahmad dan Syafi'i, mengenakan pakaian sutra dibolehkan [bagi laki-laki jika ada kebutuhan yang mengharuskannya]. Izin yang diberikan kepada sebagian kaum Muslim [seperti mengenakan pakaian sutra bagi laki-laki dalam kasus ini] karena keadaan khusus berlaku bagi semua orang yang menghadapi keadaan serupa.

Beberapa ulama mengatakan bahwa hadith-hadith yang melarang pakaian sutra bagi laki-laki berlaku secara umum. Mereka mengatakan bahwa hadith (yang diriwayatkan di atas) mungkin mengandung izin khusus hanya untuk Abdurrahman bin Awf dan Az-Zubayr saja. Mereka mengatakan bahwa mungkin juga izin ini berlaku bagi kaum Muslim lainnya. Ketika ada kemungkinan demikian, berpegang pada teks umum adalah yang lebih tepat. Inilah sebabnya sebagian perawi hadith ini berkomentar, "Aku tidak tahu apakah izin ini berlaku bagi orang-orang yang datang setelah mereka (Abdurrahman bin Awf dan Az-Zubayr)."

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

Pendapat yang benar adalah bahwa izin tersebut bersifat umum dalam indikasinya dan tidak ada dalil yang menunjukkan bahwa izin itu bersifat khusus atau terbatas. Demikian pula, Nabi ﷺ pernah berkata kepada Abu Burdah, "Izin ini berlaku untukmu saja dan tidak untuk siapa pun setelahmu." Selain itu, Allah berfirman kepada Nabi-Nya ﷺ mengenai wanita yang menawarkan dirinya untuk dinikahi oleh Nabi ﷺ:

"Sebagai kekhususan bagimu saja, bukan untuk (seluruh) orang-orang beriman."

Karena larangan mengenakan pakaian sutra bagi laki-laki merupakan tindakan pencegahan, maka dibolehkan dalam situasi tertentu dan untuk manfaat yang mengharuskannya. Misalnya, memandang wanita dilarang sebagai tindakan pencegahan, tetapi dibolehkan ketika ada kebutuhan nyata. Demikian juga halnya dengan larangan melakukan salah sunnah pada waktu-waktu tertentu [saat matahari terbit atau terbenam], untuk mencegah kemiripan sekecil apa pun antara kaum Muslim dan penyembah matahari. Namun, dalam beberapa kasus, kaum Muslim dibolehkan melakukan salah sunnah selama waktu-waktu tersebut untuk kebutuhan dan manfaat nyata. Kami telah menjelaskan kasus-kasus di mana mengenakan pakaian sutra dibolehkan dalam kitab kami tentang topik ini, At-Tabiri.

Secara medis, sutra dihasilkan oleh hewan dan merupakan obat. Sutra memiliki banyak manfaat, seperti menenangkan dan memperkuat jantung serta membantu meredakan berbagai penyakitnya. Sutra juga membantu mengatasi empedu hitam dan

penyakit apa pun yang mungkin ditimbulkannya. Lebih lanjut, sutra memperkuat penglihatan ketika digunakan sebagai celak. Sutra mentah, yang digunakan dalam menyiapkan obat-obatan dan pengobatan, bersifat panas dan kering pada tingkat pertama. Ketika sutra digunakan dalam pakaian, ia bersifat ringan dan menghangatkan tubuh. Terkadang ia juga bisa mendinginkan tubuh.

Ar-Razi berkata, "Sutra lebih panas daripada linen, lebih dingin daripada kapas dan mengembangkan daging. Setiap jenis pakaian tebal melemahkan tubuh dan mengeraskan kulit."

Ada tiga jenis pakaian, satu yang memberikan kehangatan dan juga memanaskan tubuh. Jenis pakaian lainnya memberikan kehangatan tetapi tidak memanaskan tubuh. Jenis ketiga tidak memberikan kehangatan maupun panas. Tidak ada jenis yang memberikan panas tetapi tidak memberikan kehangatan kepada pemakainya. Misalnya, pakaian yang terbuat dari wol dan bulu hewan meningkatkan suhu tubuh dan memberikan kehangatan pada kulit. Di sisi lain, pakaian sutra, Kittan (linen) dan kapas hanya memberikan kehangatan pada kulit. Pakaian Kittan bersifat dingin dan kering, pakaian wol bersifat panas dan kering, sementara pakaian kapas bersifat moderat. Pakaian sutra lebih lembut dan tidak sepanas wol. Penulis Al-Minhaj menyatakan bahwa mengenakan sutra, "Tidak memberikan kehangatan sebanyak kapas, karena sifatnya yang lebih ringan."

Setiap jenis pakaian yang lembut dan halus kurang memanaskan tubuh dan kurang efektif dalam membantu proses dekomposisi. Itulah sebabnya lebih baik jenis pakaian ini

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

dikenakan pada musim panas dan di daerah panas.

Karena pakaian sutra tidak kering dan tidak setebal jenis pakaian lainnya, pakaian sutra membantu sebagai pengobatan untuk ruam kulit yang diakibatkan oleh bahan yang kering dan tebal. Oleh karena itu, Nabi ﷺ mengizinkan Az-Zubayr dan Abdurrahman untuk mengenakan pakaian sutra karena ruam kulit mereka. Selain itu, pakaian sutra adalah yang paling tidak ramah bagi tungau atau kutu, karena bukan lingkungan terbaik tempat tungau hidup dan berkembang biak.

Jenis-jenis bahan penutup tubuh yang tidak meningkatkan suhu tubuh maupun memberikan kehangatan terbuat dari besi, timah, kayu, pasir, dan sebagainya.

Jika seseorang bertanya, "Karena sutra menyediakan jenis pakaian terbaik dan paling cocok untuk tubuh, mengapa ia dilarang bagi laki-laki oleh syariat Islam, yang merupakan syariat paling mulia dan sempurna yang membolehkan hal-hal yang baik dan suci serta hanya melarang hal-hal yang tidak baik?" Kami harus menyebutkan bahwa ini adalah pertanyaan yang kaum Muslim berbeda pendapat mengenai jawabannya.

Mereka yang menolak bahwa ada hikmah di balik syariat Islam bahkan tidak perlu menjawab pertanyaan ini.

Adapun mereka yang menegaskan bahwa ada hikmah di balik syariat Islam, yang merupakan mayoritas, mengatakan bahwa Islam melarang pakaian sutra bagi laki-laki agar mereka

bersabar dan meninggalkan memakainya karena Allah. Dalam hal ini, mereka akan mendapat pahala dari Allah, terutama karena ada jenis pakaian lain yang bisa dikenakan.

Sebagian orang mengatakan bahwa sutra diciptakan untuk manfaat wanita, seperti halnya emas. Oleh karena itu, sutra dilarang bagi laki-laki agar mereka tidak menyerupai wanita. Sebagian kaum Muslim mengatakan bahwa sutra dilarang karena menyebabkan kesombongan dan keangkuhan.

Yang lain mengatakan bahwa sutra dilarang karena kelembutan pada kulit yang menyebabkan perilaku feminin sekaligus melemahkan kejantanan dan maskulinitas. Oleh karena itu, jarang sekali terlihat seorang laki-laki yang mengenakan pakaian sutra yang tidak terpengaruh oleh kelembutannya dengan secara tidak sengaja meniru perilaku dan kelembutan feminin, meskipun ia termasuk laki-laki yang paling maskulin. Mengenakan pakaian sutra pasti akan mengurangi kualitas kejantanan dan maskulinitas, meskipun kualitas-kualitas ini tidak akan hilang seluruhnya. Adapun mereka yang tidak memahami fakta-fakta ini, hendaklah mereka tunduk pada keputusan bijak Allah. Akhirnya, kami harus menyatakan bahwa menurut pendapat yang benar, anak laki-laki kecil tidak boleh diizinkan mengenakan pakaian sutra, karena mereka akan memperoleh perilaku feminin.

An-Nasa'i meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Allah telah membolehkan sutra dan emas bagi kaum wanita dari umatku dan mengharamkannya bagi

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

kaum laki-laki dari umatku." Dalam riwayat lain, Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Memakai sutra dan emas diharamkan bagi kaum laki-laki dari umatku dan dibolehkan bagi kaum wanitanya."

Al-Bukhari juga meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarang memakai dan duduk di atas pakaian sutra dan Dibaj (pakaian sutra murni), kemudian beliau menambahkan, "Itu untuk mereka (siapa pun di antara laki-laki yang memakainya) di dunia ini dan untuk kalian di akhirat kelak."

Petunjuk Nabi dalam mengobati radang selaput dada (pleurisy)

Para dokter membagi radang selaput dada menjadi dua jenis, yaitu yang sejati dan yang tidak sejati. Jenis pertama adalah pertumbuhan yang terinfeksi yang muncul pada pleura yang melapisi tulang rusuk. Jenis kedua menyebabkan rasa sakit yang serupa dan menyerang pleura sebagai akibat dari perut kembung yang kental dan tersumbat di antara peritoneum (selaput halus transparan yang melapisi rongga perut dan sebagian daerah panggul). Rasa sakit yang disebabkan oleh radang selaput dada yang tidak sejati bersifat akut, sementara radang selaput dada yang sejati menyebabkan rasa sakit yang menusuk.

Penulis kitab Al-Qanun berkata, "Terkadang, bagian samping tubuh, peritoneum, otot dada, tulang rusuk dan daerah sekitarnya mengalami pertumbuhan yang sangat menyakitkan yang disebut radang selaput dada. Terkadang, bisa juga terjadi rasa sakit di bagian tubuh yang sama tetapi bukan akibat dari

pertumbuhan, melainkan akibat penumpukan gas-gas berbahaya (perut kembung),

Shubrum is among the medications that doctors do not advise people to use because it is a very strong

laxative. In the Haditb, النبي ﷺ sg described Shubrum as being hot and a strong laxative.

Senna is a plant that grows in AI-Hijaz (Western Arabia) and the best kind of it grows in Mecca, Senna is hot and dry in tue first degree and it is a good, mild medication that does not cause any side effects. Senno helps against bile and black bile ailments and strengthens the heart, which is another good quality that this medication contains. Senno is useful against melancholic obsession and corporal incisions, relaxes the muscles and improves the hair. Senno In addition helps against lice, headache, mange, pustules, rashes and epilepsy. It is better to cook the Senno whole and drink three measures of its soup than cooking it crushed. In addition, it is better to cook the Senno mixed with violet flowers and red raisins, after removing the seeds,

Ar-Razi said, "Senna and fumitory help extract mature humors (bodily wastes) and helps against leprosy and rashes, when one takes four to seven measures of each remedy."

As for Sonoot (cumin), there is a difference of opinion concerning its nature. Umar bin Bakr As-Saksaki, for instance, said that Sonoot is either honey or butter extract. Another opinion suggests that Sonoot is a seed that resembles the Kammon (cumin), as Ibn Al-Arabi has asserted. The fourth and fifth opinions suggest that

——-— The Prophetic Mecicine

Sonoot is the Persian cumin or fennel. according to Abu Hanifah Ad-Daynocri. The sixth opinion sugyests that it is the Shibitt (dill), and the seventh opinion suggests that it is the date, according to Abu Bakr bin As-Sunni, The eighth opinion suggests that it is honey that exists in butter-skin, according to Abdul-Latif Al-Baghdadi, Some doctors In addition stated that the last meaning is the most probable. In this case, the Sanoot is prepared by blending Senno powder with some honey mixed with ghee, as this is better than taking the Senna by itself. Then one licks the mixture that contains the Senna, as in this case the Senna taste will be made milder, because of the honey and butter, which will In addition assist the laxative power of the Senna. الله knows best.

النبي ﷺ's guidance on treating skin rashes and scabies caused by mites

In the Sahihan it is narrated that Anas said, "رسول الله ﷺ b has allowed Abdur Rahman bin Awf and Az-Zubayr bin Al'Awwam to wear silk garments because of a skin rash they had." fn another narration, "Abdur Rahman bin Awf and Az-Zubayr bin Al-Awwam complained to النبي ﷺ b during a battle that they had mites, and he allowed them to wear silk garments which I saw them wearing."

There are two areas of interest regarding this الحديث, one in the area of Figh (islamic Jurisprudence)

In the area of Figh (Islamic Jurisprudence), the السنة النبوية of رسول الله ﷺ is that wearing silken garments is allowed for women and disallowed for men, except when there is a legitimate need. For instance, [men are allowed to wear silken garments] in cold weather, when one-has ors a sitken garment to wear and when complaining from mange, rashes, mites or lice, as evident by the last الحديث.

According to Imam Ahmad and Shafi'i, wearing silken garments is allowed {for men if a necessity warrants it]. The permission given to some Muslims {such as wearing silken garments for men in this case] due to special circumstances applies te all those who face similar circumstances.

Several scholars said that the Ahadeeth that disallow silken garments for men apply in general. They said that the الحديث (narrated above) might entail a specific permission for only Abdur-Rahman bin '‘Awf and Az-Zubayr. They say that it is possible, though, that this permission applies to other Muslims. When there is a probability, adhering to the general text is warranted. This is why some of the narrators of this الحديث commented, "I do not know if the permission applies to whoever comes after them (Abdur-Rahman bin Awf and Az-Zubayr)."

——— The Prophetic Medicine

The correct opinion is that the permission is general in its indication and that there is no evidence that it is specific or restricted. Similarly, النبي ﷺ st once said to Abu Burdah, "This permission applies to you and none else after you." In addition, الله said to His Prophet * regarding the woman who offered herself in marriage to النبي ﷺ b:

"A privilege for you only, not for the (rest of) the believers."

Since disallowing wearing silken garments for men is a precautionary measure, it is allowed in certain situations and for a benefit that warrants it. For instance, gazing at women is disallowed as a precautionary measure, but it is allowed when there is a real need. Such is the case with disallowing performing voluntary prayers at certain times while the sun is rising or setting], to prevent even a hint of resemblance between Muslims and sun worshippers. Yet, in some cases, it is allowed for Muslims to offer voluntary prayers during these times for a real need and benefit. We have explained the cases when wearing silken garments is allowed in our book on this subject, At-Jabiri.

Medically, silk is produced by an animal and isa remedy. Silk has many benefits, such as soothing and strengthening the heart and helping relieve several of its ailments. Silk In addition helps against black bile and

whatever ailments it might cause. Further, silk strengthens the eyesight when used as eyeliner. Raw silk, which is used in preparing medications and remedies, is hot and dry in the first degree. When silk is used in clothes, it is mild and heats the body. Sometimes it could chill the body.

Ar-Razi said, "Silk is hotter than linen, colder than cotton and develops the flesh. Every type of thick clothes weakens the body and hardens the skin."

There are three types of clothes, one that brings warmth and In addition heats the body. Another type of clothes brings warmth but no heat to the body. The third type does not bring warmth or heat. There is no type that brings heat but not warmth to those wearing it. For instance, clothes made of wool and animal hair elevate body temperature and bring warmth to the skin. On the other hand, silk, Kittan (linen) and cotton garments only bring warmth to the skin. Kittan clothes are cold and dry, wool clothes are hot and dry, while cotton garments are moderate. Silken garments are softer and less hot than wool. The author of Al-Minhaj stated that wearing silk, "Does not bring as much warmth as cotton, because it is milder."

Every type of soft, polished clothes heat the body less and is less effective in helping the decomposition process. That is why it is better that this type of clothes is

———— The Prophetic Medicine

worn during summer and in hot areas.

Since silk.n garments are neither dry nor thick as the other types of clothes, they help as a treatment for skin rashes that result from dry, thick material. Hence, النبي ﷺ “ allowed Az-Zubair and Abdur-Rahman to wear silken garments due to their skin rash. In addition, silken garments are the least hospitable to mites or lice, for it is not the best environment where mites live and thrive.

The types of body wear that neither elevates the body temperature nor brings warmth are made of iron, lead, wood, sand, and so forth.

If one asks, "Since silk provides the best type of clothes and the most suitable for the body, why was it prohibited for men) by the Islamic legislation, which is the mot honorable, perfect legislation and which allows the good and pure things and only disallows the impure things?" We should mention that this is a question that the Muslims differ regarding its answer.

Those who deny that there is wisdom behind the Islamic legislation do not even need to answer this question.

As for those who affirm that there is a wisdom behind the Islamic legislation, who are the majority, say that Islam disallowed silken garments for men so that they

observe patience and abandon wearing them for الله's Sake. In this case, they will be rewarded by الله, especially since there are other types of clothes to wear.

Some people say that silk was created for the benefit of women, such as the case with gold. Hence, silk is prohibited for men so that they do not imitate women. Some Muslims say that silk was prohibited because it leads to arrogance and pride.

Others say that silk was disallowed because of its sofiness on the skin that leads to feminine behavior while weakening manhed and masculinity. Hence, you rarely see a man wearing silken garments who is not affected by its softness by inadvertently imitating feminine behavior and softness, even if he was among the most masculine men. Wearing silken garments will certainly diminish the manly qualities and masculinity, although these qualities will not disappear all together. As for those who do not comprehend these facts, let them submit to الله's wise decisions. Finally, we should state that according to the correct opinion, young boys should not be allowed to wear silken garments, because they will acquire feminine behavior.

An-Nasa'y narrated that النبي ﷺ b said:

"الله has allowed silk and gold for the females of my Ummah (Muslim Nation) and disallowed them for the

——— The Prophetic Medicine

males of my Ummah (Muslim Nation)." In another narration, النبي ﷺ b said:

"Wearing silk and gold is disallowed for the males of my Nation and allowed for the females."

Al-Bokhary In addition narrated that رسول الله ﷺ b has disallowed wearing and sitting on silken garments and Dibaj (pure silken clothes), and then added, "It is for them (whoever wears them among men) in this life and for you in the Life Hereafter."

النبي ﷺ's guidance on treating pleurisy

The doctors divide pleurisy into two types, real and unreal. The first type is an infected growth that appears in the pleura that coats the ribs. The second type causes a similar pain and attacks the pleura as a result of thick, congested flatulence between the peritoneum's (smooth transparent membrane that lines the abdominal cavity and part of the pelvic region). The pain caused by the unreal pleurisy is acute while the real pleurisy causes stinging pain.

The author of the Qonoon said, "Sometimes, the flank (of the body), the peritoneum, chest muscles and ribs and surrounding areas suffer from an extremely painful growth called pleurisy. Sometimes, there could be pain in these same areas of the body but not as a result of

growth but of accumulating harmful gasses (flatulence),
